articles.headline: 7 Reasons Why The F-22 Still Outperforms Newer Jets
articles.blurb: While the F-35 now outperforms F-22 as a balanced advanced fighter, the F-22 still wins in its air dominance role and some flying characteristics.
articles.takeaway: The F-22's specialized air dominance capabilities remain unmatched despite the F-35's broader multi-role advantages, highlighting how role specialization can trump generational advancement.

articles.headline: How to Assess Safety Risk
articles.blurb: A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.
articles.takeaway: Part 5's structured risk assessment framework enables operators to systematically identify and quantify hazards before they escalate into accidents or violations.

articles.headline: Why Do Cargo Airlines Still Prefer The Boeing 747 Over Newer Jets?
articles.blurb: The new generation of jets has pushed out the icons of old, but one is still hanging around and is actually preferred over many of these new options.
articles.takeaway: The 747's unmatched upper-deck cargo capacity and established airport infrastructure justify its preference despite higher fuel costs versus modern widebodies.

articles.headline: "Possible Hijacking": Frontier Passenger Triggers Security Scare After Bomb And Death Threats
articles.blurb: A level four alert on flight 2539 in Atlanta.
articles.takeaway: Level four security alerts for bomb threats require immediate operational protocols that can disrupt schedules and increase costs for airlines managing passenger conduct escalations.

articles.headline: Singapore Airlines Smashes Records: Now 23 Daily Australia Flights
articles.blurb: The carrier will be the first international airline to serve Sydney's new airport.
articles.takeaway: Singapore Airlines' expanded Australia capacity signals strong post-pandemic demand recovery and competitive positioning in the Asia-Pacific route network.

articles.headline: Safety Culture in Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.
articles.takeaway: Safety Management Systems succeed or fail based on organizational willingness to report hazards without fear—a cultural shift that requires leadership commitment beyond compliance requirements.

articles.headline: Indonesia receives final A400M after rerouted delivery flight around Middle East

articles.takeaway: Indonesia's A400M delivery required Middle East routing adjustments, highlighting geopolitical constraints affecting military aircraft logistics and supply chain resilience in the Indo-Pacific region.

articles.headline: What Happens To Aircraft After Retirement?
articles.blurb: The fate of retired commercial jets is a mystery to many, but in reality, there are many interesting journeys that they can go on.
articles.takeaway: Retired aircraft create a secondary market spanning cargo conversion, leasing, and scrapping, generating significant residual value and extending aircraft utility for 20+ years beyond initial service.

articles.headline: The Striking Differences Between The Airbus A321XLR & Boeing 737 MAX 10
articles.blurb: The Airbus A321XLR and Boeing 737 MAX 10 represent the pinnacle of evolution in the aviation industry, pushing narrowbodies to their absolute limits.
articles.takeaway: The A321XLR's 4,700nm range and larger fuselage enable airlines to profitably operate thin routes previously requiring widebodies, directly challenging the 737 MAX 10's market positioning.

articles.headline: Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

articles.takeaway: Wire strike fatalities persist because pilots overestimate their reaction time, making proactive route planning and terrain awareness training critical for helicopter safety.

articles.blurb: The carrier is set to serve three European destinations this year.
articles.takeaway: Alaska Airlines' European expansion signals a major shift in the carrier's strategic positioning from regional to transcontinental competitor.

articles.headline: Disruptive Passenger Forces American Airlines Boeing 737 MAX 8 To Divert To Detroit

articles.takeaway: Escalating unruly passenger incidents are creating operational costs through diversions while simultaneously straining crew resources and generating regulatory scrutiny for carriers.

articles.headline: Why Do Airlines Turn Off The Cabin Lights During Takeoff And Landing?
articles.blurb: This practice ensures that passengers are better prepared during an emergency.
articles.takeaway: Dimmed cabin lighting during critical phases enables passengers' eyes to adjust to darkness, accelerating evacuation speed if emergency slides are deployed.

articles.headline: Here’s How US Airline Economy Seat Pitch Compares In 2026
articles.blurb: Even an extra inch of seat pitch can make all the difference to passenger comfort.
articles.takeaway: Most US carriers have stabilized economy seat pitch around 31 inches for 2026, though ultra-low-cost carriers continue aggressive compression that reduces comfort and increases potential health risks on longer routes.

articles.headline: Misawa’s 13th Fighter Squadron Receives First F-35s
articles.blurb: Misawa Air Base has received the U.S. Air Force’s first F-35A Lightning II fighters to be permanently based in Japan, beginning the transition from the F-16 to the fifth-gen aircraft. Misawa Air Base, home of the U.S. Air Force’s 35th Fighter Wing, has received its first four F-35A Lightning II...
articles.takeaway: Japan's first permanent F-35A deployment strengthens U.S. regional deterrence capabilities while accelerating the retirement timeline for older F-16 platforms across Pacific operations.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport systems offers immediate emissions reduction potential while addressing capacity constraints ahead of long-term technology transitions.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Press Release: Aer Lingus takes off with Starlink inflight Wi-Fi

Customers travelling from Dublin to New York JFK on flight EI105 today are the first to experience the innovative...
articles.takeaway: Aer Lingus's Starlink integration on transatlantic routes enables consistent high-speed connectivity across oceanic regions where traditional air-to-ground systems are unavailable, creating competitive differentiation in premium long-haul markets.

articles.takeaway: Seat design is evolving from a structural component into a critical infrastructure hub for power distribution and connectivity systems, requiring manufacturers to rethink electrical architecture.

articles.headline: ‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings
articles.blurb: While NASA outlined plans to increase the cadence of robotic lunar lander missions at a recent event, it said little about accelerating work on crewed lunar landers.
articles.takeaway: NASA is prioritizing rapid robotic lunar exploration over accelerated crewed missions, suggesting a phased approach that may extend the human return-to-Moon timeline.

articles.headline: Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission
articles.blurb: Starfish Space has changed plans for a docking demonstration mission after its original partner backed out.
articles.takeaway: Starfish Space's pivot demonstrates the fragility of nascent orbital servicing partnerships and the operational flexibility required in emerging space logistics markets.

articles.headline: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC
